text: Law Society of Ontario 2020 Annual Report
In 2020, the number of new complaints received in Professional Regulation was 9% lower than the number
received in 2019 and 5.1% lower than the number received in 2018.
Year Number of new complaints received
With respect to the 3,987 new complaints received in Professional Regulation in 2020, the breakdown by
subject and by complainant are consistent with past years.
The remaining complaints/cases involved non-licensees and lawyer and paralegal applicants. •
74% of complaints were brought by members of the public •
12% of complaints were brought by licensees •
14% of complaints were internally raised (i.e., the complainant is the Law Society). •
The types of allegations raised in complaints received in Professional Regulation and those complaints
transferred to Investigations in 2020 were similar to the types of allegations raised in these two groups in the
past few years. With respect to the underlying complaints in the notices filed in 2020, there were more
governance issues raised than in previous years.

Areas of law raised in complaints
Note that these statistics include only complaints in which at least one area of law was identified. More than
one area of law can be identified in a complaint.
The areas of law raised in complaints received in Professional Regulation, complaints transferred to
Investigations and the underlying complaints in notices filed with the Tribunal in 2020 were similar to the
areas of law raised in these groups in the past few years.

Hearings before the Law Society Tribunal – Hearing Division
In 2020, 165 notices were filed: 109 involved lawyers; 42 involved paralegals; 7 involved paralegal

(including conduct, capacity and interlocutory suspension or applicants
(including motions to vary or reinstatement and terms
Note that a “completed hearing” for Professional Regulation is defined as one in which the Law Society
Tribunal – Hearing Division has rendered a final order and the Tribunal is considered functus or when a
matter has been withdrawn, abandoned or deemed abandoned.
In 2020, a total of 123 hearings were completed before the Law Society Tribunal – Hearing Division: 90
involving lawyers/lawyer applicants; 33 involving paralegals/paralegal applicants.
